channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Release of remaining Mandelson files anticipated next week
item.description: The anticipated release of the final tranche of files related to Peter Mandelson has been scheduled for next week. However, due to restricted access to the original source, specific details within the...

item.title: Federal Court rules Coles misled shoppers with misleading discount campaign
item.description: The Federal Court has ruled that Coles misled consumers with its Down Down pricing campaign, labelling it as providing 'illusory discounts'. Justice Michael O’Bryan found that 13 out of 14 price ticke...

item.title: Boko Haram Executes Captives Who Tried to Escape in Borno
item.description: Na serious wahala dey ground for Borno State as one faction of Boko Haram wey dem sabi as Jama’atu Ahlis-Sunna Lidda’Awati Wal-Jihad (JAS) don behead more than seven people wey try run comot from thei...

item.title: Truck Driver Sentenced for Fatal Crash That Killed Former Olympic Skater Alexandra Paul
item.description: On May 13, 2026, a truck driver in Ontario was sentenced to two and a half years in prison after pleading guilty to dangerous driving charges that resulted in the death of former Canadian Olympic figu...

item.title: Power Grids in Luzon and Visayas Face Alerts, Causing Rolling Blackouts
item.description: Thousands of households and businesses in Luzon and the Visayas experienced power interruptions yesterday following the issuance of yellow and red alerts by the National Grid Corp. of the Philippines ...

item.title: Congress set to announce Kerala Chief Minister following assembly election victory
item.description: Following the Kerala assembly elections held recently, the Congress party, which secured 63 seats out of the 102 contested under the United Democratic Front (UDF) banner, is expected to announce the n...

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro Addresses Allegations Regarding Request for Funds from Banker
item.description: Brazilian senator and presidential hopeful Flávio Bolsonaro has publicly denied any wrongdoing following reports that he requested a significant sum of money from a banker. The controversy has drawn s...

item.title: TPDC and PAET Discuss Gas Licence Issues at Songo Songo
item.description: The Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) and Pan African Energy Tanzania (PAET) have started talks over the Songo Songo gas licence. These discussions are aimed at resolving pending issue...

item.title: Jeffries Criticizes Republican Gerrymandering and Voter Suppression Efforts
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) addressed ongoing issues of racial and political inequity in the U.S., warning that the legacy of the Confederacy continues to influence contemporary gov...

item.title: Ex-AGF Malami and family face trial over N8.7bn money laundering case
item.description: For real, the case wey dey Federal High Court Abuja don dey hot. Former Attorney-General of the Federation, Abubakar Malami, him wife Asabe Bashir, and their son Abdulaziz Malami dey face 16 charges o...

item.title: PCSO announces no jackpot winners for May 13, 2026 lotto draws
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) confirmed that there were no winners for the major lotto jackpot draws held on Wednesday, May 13, 2026. For the Grand Lotto 6/55, which had a jackpot p...

item.title: Alberta Court Quashes Independence Petition Due to First Nations Treaty Concerns
item.description: An Alberta Court of King’s Bench justice has nullified Elections Alberta's approval of a petition seeking a referendum on Alberta independence, initiated by the separatist group Stay Free Alberta. The...

item.title: Wes Streeting plans to initiate Labour leadership contest amid party divisions
item.description: Wes Streeting, the health secretary, is reportedly preparing to launch a challenge to Keir Starmer's leadership of the Labour Party, provided he secures the backing of enough MPs. This potential leade...

item.title: Supreme Court allows TVK MLA to vote in Tamil Nadu floor test, stays Madras HC order
item.description: In a crucial development ahead of the Tamil Nadu assembly floor test, the Supreme Court on Wednesday allowed MLA Srinivasa Sethupathi of the TVK party to participate, staying the Madras High Court ord...

item.title: PM Modi Urges Work From Home Voluntarily; No Official Mandate Issued, Say Sources
item.description: According to government sources, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed to citizens and businesses to consider working from home where feasible, as a precautionary measure. However, there is no for...

item.title: Iheanacho’s Last-Minute Penalty Keeps Celtic in Scottish Premiership Title Race Against Hearts
item.description: Na so drama take dey for Scottish Premiership this season. Kelechi Iheanacho score one controversial penalty for 99th minute wey carry Celtic go win 3-2 against Motherwell. Dis win mean say Celtic sti...

item.title: Police confiscate five kilos of shabu worth P34 million in Zamboanga City operation
item.description: Authorities in Zamboanga City successfully confiscated five kilos of shabu valued at P34 million during an entrapment operation on May 12, 2026. The operation, conducted in the underground parking are...

item.title: Tripura government orders 50% work-from-home for employees following PM’s appeal
item.description: In response to an appeal made by the Prime Minister, the Tripura government has decided to implement a 50 percent work-from-home policy for its employees. This step has been taken as a precautionary m...

item.title: Rising Risks from Reentering Space Debris Due to Advanced Spacecraft Materials
item.description: As space launches increase worldwide, reentering space debris is becoming a growing safety concern. Components like nonreusable rocket boosters and decommissioned satellites are meant to burn up upon ...

item.title: KUSCCO Faces Sh12 Billion Deficit Following Years of Financial Mismanagement, Says Oparanya
item.description: Co-operatives and MSMEs Development Cabinet Secretary Wycliffe Oparanya has disclosed that the Kenya Union of Savings and Credit Co-operatives (KUSCCO) is experiencing a massive financial crisis estim...
